docs.title: About SportsLabResearch
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch is an independent research initiative focused on the development of scientific software, hardware and digital technologies for Sports Science.

The project was created to establish a unified ecosystem where research software, educational resources, hardware developments and scientific documentation share the same quality standards, development methodology and visual identity.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: Develop robust, reliable and reproducible scientific software following international research software engineering standards.

docs.title: Vision
docs.text: To become an international reference in research software for Sport Sciences through innovation, scientific quality and open science.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
• Scientific Excellence
  • Reproducible Research
  • Open Science
  • FAIR Principles
  • Software Quality
  • Continuous Validation
  • Long-Term Sustainability

docs.title: Research Software
docs.text: Development of scientific applications for research and professional practice.

docs.title: Artificial Intelligence
docs.text: Machine learning, computer vision and intelligent decision support systems.

docs.title: Wearable Technologies
docs.text: Heart rate variability, physiological monitoring, GPS, IMU and sports sensors.

docs.title: Sports Analytics
docs.text: Performance analysis, data science and predictive analytics.

docs.title: Arduino & Embedded Systems
docs.text: Educational and research platforms based on open hardware.

docs.title: Scientific Documentation
docs.text: Professional documentation using MkDocs and GitHub.

docs.title: Ecosystem
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch integrates:

  • Scientific Software
  • Hardware Projects
  • Educational Resources
  • Research Frameworks
  • Publications
  • Datasets
  • Technical Documentation

docs.title: Development Philosophy
docs.text:
Every project developed under SportsLabResearch follows the same workflow:

  1. Scientific idea
  2. Software design
  3. Development
  4. Scientific validation
  5. Documentation
  6. Publication
  7. Continuous improvement

docs.title: Commitment
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch promotes transparent, reproducible and sustainable scientific software designed to support researchers, lecturers and professionals worldwide.

Research Software engineered for the future of Sport Science.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: SportsLabResearch develops open, reproducible and scientifically validated research software for Sport Science, Health and Human Performance.

docs.title: Collaborations
docs.text:
We welcome:

  • Research collaborations

  • Scientific consulting

docs.title: SportsLabResearch Framework
docs.text: The SportsLabResearch Framework defines the common methodology followed by every software and hardware project developed within the ecosystem.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Research
docs.text:
• Problem identification
  • Literature review
  • Scientific objectives
  • Study design

docs.title: Phase 2 — Design
docs.text:
• Software architecture
  • Data model
  • User workflow
  • Interface design

docs.title: Phase 3 — Development
docs.text:
• Modular programming
  • Version control
  • Code quality
  • Documentation

docs.title: Phase 4 — Validation
docs.text:
Every project must include scientific validation whenever applicable.

Validation may include:

  • Reliability
  • Accuracy
  • Precision
  • Agreement analysis
  • Statistical analysis
  • Benchmark testing

docs.title: Phase 5 — Documentation
docs.text:
Each project includes:

  • README
  • User Guide
  • Technical Documentation
  • Changelog
  • License
  • Citation file

docs.title: Phase 6 — Publication
docs.text:
Projects are disseminated through:

  • GitHub
  • GitHub Pages
  • Zenodo
  • Scientific Journals
  • Conference Communications

docs.title: Phase 7 — Maintenance
docs.text:
• Continuous improvement
  • Bug fixing
  • New releases
  • Scientific updates

docs.title: Core Values
docs.text:
• Scientific Excellence
  • Transparency
  • Reproducibility
  • Open Science
  • FAIR Principles
  • Long-Term Sustainability

docs.title: Standard Project Structure
docs.text:
Project
│
├── docs
├── src
├── data
├── tests
├── assets
├── config
├── results
├── README.md
├── LICENSE
├── CHANGELOG.md
├── CITATION.cff
└── mkdocs.yml

Every project. Same methodology. Same scientific standards.

docs.title: Publications
docs.text: SportsLabResearch promotes the dissemination of scientific knowledge through publications, research software, technical documentation and open science resources.

docs.title: Scientific Articles
docs.text:
Research articles published in peer-reviewed journals covering:

  • Sports Performance
  • Exercise Physiology
  • Wearable Technologies
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Sports Analytics
  • Research Software

docs.title: Research Software
docs.text:
Every software project is documented and versioned following international research software engineering practices.

Software releases include:

  • Source code
  • Documentation
  • Release notes
  • Citation information
  • Version history

docs.title: Technical Reports
docs.text:
Technical reports provide detailed descriptions of:

  • Algorithms
  • Validation studies
  • Benchmark analyses
  • Software architecture
  • Experimental procedures

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text:
Whenever possible, datasets are published following FAIR principles.

Datasets include:

  • Documentation
  • Metadata
  • Version information
  • Usage guidelines

docs.title: Educational Resources
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch also develops educational material for:

  • University teaching
  • Research training
  • Professional development
  • Workshops
  • Practical laboratories

docs.text:

docs.text: If you use SportsLabResearch software in your research, please cite the corresponding repository and associated publication when available.

docs.title: Scientific Standards
docs.text: Every project developed under SportsLabResearch follows common scientific and software engineering standards to ensure quality, reproducibility and long-term sustainability.

docs.title: Research Standards
docs.text:
• Evidence-based development
  • Scientific methodology
  • Transparent reporting
  • Reproducible research
  • Ethical research practices

docs.title: Software Standards
docs.text:
Every project includes:

  • Modular architecture
  • Version control (Git)
  • Professional documentation
  • CHANGELOG
  • LICENSE
  • CITATION.cff
  • Continuous maintenance

docs.title: Validation Standards
docs.text:
Whenever applicable, projects should report:

  • Validity
  • Reliability
  • Accuracy
  • Precision
  • Agreement
  • Benchmark comparison
  • Statistical evaluation

docs.title: Documentation Standards
docs.text:
Each repository includes:

  • README
  • User Guide
  • Technical Documentation
  • API Documentation (when applicable)
  • Examples
  • Release Notes

docs.title: Open Science Standards
docs.text:
SportsLabResearch promotes:

  • Open-source software
  • FAIR principles
  • Reproducible workflows
  • Transparent development
  • Open documentation

docs.title: Development Standards
docs.text:
The recommended workflow is:

Research

↓

Design

↓

Development

↓

Validation

↓

Documentation

↓

Publication

↓

Maintenance

docs.title: Quality Checklist
docs.text:
Every project should satisfy the following checklist before a public release:

Item Required README ✅ Documentation ✅ License ✅ CHANGELOG ✅ Citation File ✅ Version Number ✅ Validation ✅ Examples ✅ GitHub Repository ✅

Quality is not an option; it is the foundation of every SportsLabResearch project.
